records.ety: [OE. worthi, wurþi, from worth, wurþ, n.; cf. Icel. verðugr, D. waardig, G. würdig, OHG. wirdīg. See Worth, n.]

defs: Having worth or excellence; possessing merit; valuable; deserving; estimable; excellent; virtuous.
quotes: Full worthy was he in his lordes war.
These banished men that I have kept withal Are men endued with worthy qualities.
Happier thou mayst be, worthier canst not be.
This worthy mind should worthy things embrace.

defs: Having suitable, adapted, or equivalent qualities or value; -- usually with of before the thing compared or the object; more rarely, with a following infinitive instead of of, or with that; as, worthy of, equal in excellence, value, or dignity to; entitled to; meriting; -- usually in a good sense, but sometimes in a bad one.
quotes: No, Warwick, thou art worthy of the sway.
The merciless Macdonwald, Worthy to be a rebel.
Whose shoes I am not worthy to bear.
And thou art worthy that thou shouldst not know More happiness.
The lodging is well worthy of the guest.

defs: Of high station; of high social position.

A man of eminent worth or value; one distinguished for useful and estimable qualities; a person of conspicuous desert; -- much used in the plural; as, the worthies of the church; political worthies; military worthies.
quotes: The blood of ancient worthies in his veins.

defs: To render worthy; to exalt into a hero.
